flashcards.front: Define Cartesian Product of two sets A and B
flashcards.back: A × B = {(a,b) : a ∈ A and b ∈ B}. It is the set of all ordered pairs where first element is from A and second is from B.

flashcards.front: What is a relation R from set A to set B?
flashcards.back: A relation R from A to B is a subset of the Cartesian product A × B, i.e., R ⊆ A × B.

flashcards.front: Define Domain and Range of a relation R
flashcards.back: Domain(R) = {a : (a,b) ∈ R for some b}. Range(R) = {b : (a,b) ∈ R for some a}.

flashcards.front: When is a relation R on set A called Reflexive?
flashcards.back: R is reflexive if for every a ∈ A, (a,a) ∈ R. Every element is related to itself.

flashcards.front: When is a relation R on set A called Symmetric?
flashcards.back: R is symmetric if (a,b) ∈ R implies (b,a) ∈ R for all a,b ∈ A.

flashcards.front: When is a relation R on set A called Transitive?
flashcards.back: R is transitive if (a,b) ∈ R and (b,c) ∈ R implies (a,c) ∈ R for all a,b,c ∈ A.
A relation that is simultaneously reflexive, symmetric, and transitive. It partitions the set into equivalence classes.

flashcards.front: What is a Partial Order Relation?
flashcards.back: A relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive. Example: ≤ relation on real numbers.

flashcards.front: When is a relation R Antisymmetric?
flashcards.back: R is antisymmetric if (a,b) ∈ R and (b,a) ∈ R implies a = b for all a,b ∈ A.

flashcards.front: Define Composition of relations R and S
flashcards.back: R ∘ S = {(a,c) : ∃b such that (a,b) ∈ S and (b,c) ∈ R}. The relation S is applied first, then R.

flashcards.front: Define Inverse of a relation R
flashcards.back: R⁻¹ = {(b,a) : (a,b) ∈ R}. It reverses the ordered pairs of R.
∅ ⊆ A × A. Contains no ordered pairs. It is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive vacuously.

flashcards.front: What is a Universal relation on set A?
flashcards.back: A × A. Contains all possible ordered pairs where both elements are from A.

flashcards.front: What is a complement of relation R?
flashcards.back: R̅ = (A × A) - R. Contains all pairs in A × A that are not in R.

flashcards.front: What is a relation matrix (adjacency matrix)?
flashcards.back: A boolean matrix M where M[i][j] = 1 if (aᵢ,aⱼ) ∈ R, else 0. Used to represent finite relations.
